Hi there —
Next week, I will be sitting down with my team to go over our priorities and strategy for the coming months. But before we do, I wanted to check in with you because it’s important to me that I get your feedback too.
While my opponent is busy begging for Donald Trump’s help to win this election, my top priority is making sure this campaign is about you and fighting for your interests in Washington.
I wish I could sit down and grab a coffee with each and every one of you to hear what’s on your mind, but with social distancing the next-best way for me to know is by emailing you a few short questions directly.
**[Will you take a minute to let me know what issues matter most to you in this race? I want to know what your priorities are and what keeps you up at night, so I really hope you’ll take a moment to fill out my survey today.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16350403/173554022/647991678?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTU1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwMzhlN2YzNC1hMjZiLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Bu1ZHXqfSaeMzaMjGUG3yAh0x5KK5PhZmRGUPLrp4tM=&emci=028e7f34-a26b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=038e7f34-a26b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=6227932)**

When I first moved to Minnesota in 1984, I met people from all walks of life with different opinions about pretty much everything, but people knew how to listen to each other. Even if they disagreed, folks were willing to respect each others' opinions.
Too often, our leaders in Washington don’t have the same penchant for listening as Minnesotans do. That’s just not how I do things.
I believe we must listen to each other and work hand in hand if we intend to take back the Senate and keep Minnesota from flipping red. But the only way we’ll win is if we set our priorities _together_ and follow through on them _together._

Your feedback is important.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16350405/173554024/647991678?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTU1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwMzhlN2YzNC1hMjZiLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Bu1ZHXqfSaeMzaMjGUG3yAh0x5KK5PhZmRGUPLrp4tM=&emci=028e7f34-a26b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=038e7f34-a26b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=6227932)**

name: Senator Jeff Merkley
subject: What I'm doing in the US Senate to fight for you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jeff Merkley for Oregon
body:
| [](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2eafb/6cd27893/5380f3a9/1590393e/2039370520/VEsH/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://www.jeffmerkley.com")
---

| | In this moment of urgency and crisis, it's critical that the federal government step up first and foremost to address the needs of the families that are most impacted. We can't let the government response to this emergency be simply bailing out powerful and well-connected industries while working families and small businesses are crushed. It's early but we've had some successes. Elizabeth Warren and I called for a nationwide moratorium on evictions and foreclosures, and the Department of Housing and Urban Development just agreed to do that for the properties they control -- protecting some 8 million families. When it comes a stimulus package, my priorities are these:
1. We must prioritize helping working families and small businesses weather this storm, not help the wealthy and well-connected.
2. We must ensure that no one is in danger of losing basic utilities -- power, water, telecommunications, and broadband.
3. If we do provide help to major corporations, we must demand good behavior in return: paid sick leave, no stock buybacks, maintain payrolls.
4. We must do everything we can to support our health care system and our medical first responders.

This crisis demands that we set aside partisanship and do what's best for the American people. I'm pushing with everything I've got to get that done. Stay tuned. clean_body: | "" | | In this moment of urgency and crisis, it's critical that the federal government step up first and foremost to address the needs of the families that are most impacted. We can't let the government response to this emergency be simply bailing out powerful and well-connected industries while working families and small businesses are crushed. It's early but we've had some successes. Elizabeth Warren and I called for a nationwide moratorium on evictions and foreclosures, and the Department of Housing and Urban Development just agreed to do that for the properties they control -- protecting some 8 million families. When it comes a stimulus package, my priorities are these: 1. We must prioritize helping working families and small businesses weather this storm, not help the wealthy and well-connected. 2. We must ensure that no one is in danger of losing basic utilities -- power, water, telecommunications, and broadband. 3. If we do provide help to major corporations, we must demand good behavior in return: paid sick leave, no stock buybacks, maintain payrolls. 4. We must do everything we can to support our health care system and our medical first responders. This crisis demands that we set aside partisanship and do what's best for the American people. I'm pushing with everything I've got to get that done. Stay tuned. More later. subject: Five Stories President Trump Doesn’t Want You to Miss
body:
Trump Signs Coronavirus Aid Package with Paid Sick Leave, Free Testing | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b480cb83a5&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| **_FIVE STORIES PRESIDENT TRUMP DOESN'T WANT YOU TO MISS_**
---
| | | [**Trump Signs Coronavirus Aid Package with Paid Sick Leave, Free Testing**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=74e5ecaad5&e=3ee4985eea)
-_The Hill_
“President Trump on Wednesday signed into law a multibillion-dollar emergency aid package aimed at helping Americans impacted by the coronavirus,” Morgan Chalfant reports. The bipartisan measure, resulting from negotiations between the Trump Administration and Congress, “includes provisions offering paid leave benefits for Americans, bolstered unemployment benefits and free diagnostic testing for the virus.”

**🎬****President Trump:** [“I want to thank all Americans for coming together”](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=fed8296d44&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| [**Vice President Pence: We Need the American People’s Help to Fight Coronavirus Now**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ff35b819c2&e=3ee4985eea)
-_USA Today_
“Now is a critical time. As the President said on Monday, over the next two weeks, there are actions that every American can and should take to help us slow the spread of the coronavirus. If we succeed in limiting the number of new cases, then we can dedicate more resources to the most vulnerable and save lives,” the Vice President writes.

**🇺🇸****Do your part:** [15 days to slow the spread](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=69a56492bc&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| [**With the Coronavirus Pandemic, Trump Administration’s True Leadership on Display**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=826270d3f5&e=3ee4985eea)
-_Washington Examiner_
“This week, one of President Trump’s most outspoken critics, Gov. Gavin Newsom of California, said this about the president and his team’s response to the cruise ship in Oakland: ‘Every single thing [Trump] said, they followed through on. Every single thing,’” Reps. Dr. Roger Marshall (R-KS) and Dr. Brian Babin (R-TX) write.
---
|
---
| [**Crises Demand the (Completely Constitutional) Use of Major Presidential Power**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7d43cb6c8c&e=3ee4985eea)
-_New York Post_
President Trump has taken strong actions to “address supply shortages, help the financially vulnerable and see us through this,” Josh Hammer writes. “Trump’s use of a wartime law to mitigate public-health concerns amid an outbreak accords with [a long] tradition of presidential leadership.”
---
|
---
| [**No Need to Hoard: There’s Plenty of Food in the System**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=307bff8485&e=3ee4985eea)
-_Politico_
“Don’t be fooled by the barren grocery store shelves: There’s plenty more food on the way. Meat, dairy and produce groups as well as federal regulators say the U.S. has an ample amount of products in cold storage to handle the unexpected demand for food and household products from Americans,” Ryan McCrimmon and Catherine Boudreau report.

**🛒****Agriculture Secretary Sonny Perdue:** [Our food supply chain is strong!](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6d12837657&e=3ee4985eea)

Mitch and his Republican colleagues in the Senate have made it clear that the people only they’re interested in serving are themselves and their corporate donors.

We started this campaign because our country can’t move forward unless we win back the Senate.
Mitch and his Republican colleagues in the Senate have made it clear that the people only they’re interested in serving are themselves and their corporate donors.
**Here’s the latest evidence:** we just found out that two Republican Senators used their power & access to information in the Senate to sell off millions of dollars worth of stocks before the coronavirus pandemic took off.

name: Rita Hart
subject: Staying safe
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rita Hart for Iowa
body:
| | | [](https://act.myngp.com/el/yvAjxtc67EdnoYuw0vhpQrnjdGGQ_knUlGh_D9zpoEU=/m9OMU3CJxQIrfgRDiyLpq4igTJQhoS5J5PqUfZGqFHY=) Peter, I hope this message finds you safe and healthy. It is an uncertain time for all of us. I am disappointed that I am not able to report back to you today from the Iowa Democratic Party county conventions, but the IDP made the right decision cancelling them last week to keep Iowans safe. We all have a role to play right now to help our communities. Practicing social distancing and good hygiene like washing our hands will help "flatten the curve" and keep our health care professionals and hospitals from being overwhelmed. I hope you will join Team Hart in following the CDC's recommendations: [https://www.cdc.gov/coronavirus/2019-ncov/prepare/prevention.html](https://act.myngp.com/el/yvAjxtc67EdnoYuw0vhpQrnjdGGQ_knUlGh_D9zpoEU=/LY7VFDZ6TnkFRKJAkg_iZXOgda4hcFFBYYTnaVdSAhw=) We know that many families are worried not just about the health impacts, but also the economic impacts. Iowans need relief and they need it now. There have been some positive steps, but there is more to do. We should expect action from our leaders to:
* Act to prevent a shortfall of critical medical supplies as there are more cases
* Support small businesses that are having to close or seeing less business as people practice social distancing
* Ensure paid family leave for all workers so that people are not choosing between working when they are sick and providing for their families
* Provide resources that make it easier for schools to provide instruction at home and ensure food security for families while schools are closed
* Create support systems for parents who are in need of child care

This is certainly not an exhaustive list. We need to all contribute to solving this crisis and we need our leaders to put politics aside and act. We know our effort is going to be fundamentally different going forward. But the central message of this team will not change. Now, more than ever, we need leadership that listens to every voice, not just the loud and powerful interests in Washington. We need leadership that listens, then cares enough to act. Thank you so much for your support and stay safe. -Rita | Paid for by Rita Hart for Iowa
---
Rita Hart for Iowa
P.O. Box 333
Wheatland IA 52777 United States

| **For Immediate Release **
**March 21, 2020 **
**_Lindstrom Campaign Aids Food Drive_
_Facilitates donation of 20,000 packages of split peas_**

Overland Park – Republican U.S. Senate candidate Dave Lindstrom announced today a donation of 20,000 packages of split peas by Kansas processor 21st Century Bean Processing Co-Op to the Kansas Republican Party sponsored food drive benefiting Catholic Charities.

“I want to thank 21st Century Bean Processing Co-Op for their generous donation to the Kansas Republican Party - Catholic Charities food drive effort,” said Republican US Senate candidate David Lindstrom. “People throughout Kansas are struggling during this difficult time and this is a great example of how we can come together to help our neighbors.”

Located in Sharon Springs, Kansas, 21st Century Bean Processing has since 1998 provided a wide variety of wholesale dry beans, peas, and bean flour. Owned by Kansas farmers, 21st Century Bean Processing is a premier provider of quality Kansas agricultural products. The donation of split peas will provide a needed rich natural source of protein for those in need and can be used in numerous family meal recipes.

“I would also like to thank Kansas Republican Party Chairman Mike Kuckelman and former State Representative Amanda Grosserode for their efforts to organize this successful food drive effort,” Lindstrom said.

###

name: Bob Menendez
subject: Hey, here's how you can help in Cresco during the coronavirus outbreak
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MENENDEZ FOR SENATE
body:
Friend --

As the coronavirus pandemic continues, many of the most vulnerable populations are facing challenges beyond immediate medical concerns.

And in such trying times, it's understandable that many might not know how they can help those in need.

The good news is, there are many different ways to assist our neighbors, as well as communities across the country, while still doing our part to halt the spread of coronavirus.

One of the ways to help is by donating to a COVID-19 Food Response Fund.

**If you're able, please donate to[Feeding America's COVID-19 Response Fund](https://go.menendezfornj.com/page/m/74542e65/74d715b9/1af38718/1f4ab85f/2182037087/VEsH/?g=NDtPN8Qot1BcghgJFwVmbpA "https://secure.feedingamerica.org/site/Donation2;jsessionid=00000000.app20122b?28371.donation=form1&idb=155356515&df_id=28371&mfc_pref=T&NONCE_TOKEN=1F90F54D58D3F6FF47E2C9F325DF264D&s_onsite_promo=hp_slider&s_subsrc=https://www.feedingamerica.org/about-us/press-room&28371_donation=form1&s_src=W203ORGSC&s_referrer=google&_ga=2.184547238.1038545703.1584545003-1176301857.1584545003&noskip=true") or [Meals on Wheels COVID-19 Response Fund](https://go.menendezfornj.com/page/m/74542e65/74d715b9/1af38718/1f4ab840/2182037087/VEsE/?g=NDtPN8Qot1BcghgJFwVmbpA "https://ams.mealsonwheelsamerica.org/eweb/DynamicPage.aspx?WebCode=covid19donate&noskip=true") now.** [FEEDING AMERICA COVID-19 RESPONSE FUND](https://go.menendezfornj.com/page/m/74542e65/74d715b9/1af38718/1f4ab85f/2182037087/VEsF/?g=NDtPN8Qot1BcghgJFwVmbpA "https://secure.feedingamerica.org/site/Donation2;jsessionid=00000000.app20122b?28371.donation=form1&idb=155356515&df_id=28371&mfc_pref=T&NONCE_TOKEN=1F90F54D58D3F6FF47E2C9F325DF264D&s_onsite_promo=hp_slider&s_subsrc=https://www.feedingamerica.org/about-us/press-room&28371_donation=form1&s_src=W203ORGSC&s_referrer=google&_ga=2.184547238.1038545703.1584545003-1176301857.1584545003&noskip=true") [MEALS ON WHEELS COVID-19 RESPONSE FUND](https://go.menendezfornj.com/page/m/74542e65/74d715b9/1af38718/1f4ab840/2182037087/VEsC/?g=NDtPN8Qot1BcghgJFwVmbpA "https://ams.mealsonwheelsamerica.org/eweb/DynamicPage.aspx?WebCode=covid19donate&noskip=true") Food banks, nursing homes, homeless shelters, and other social institutions are dealing with additional challenges as they attempt to social distance while still trying to secure food and resources.

As a part of the _Families First Coronavirus Response Act_ that was just signed into law, our government is assisting these efforts with:

* $500 million to WIC programs across the country to provide nutritious foods to low-income pregnant women or mothers with young children impacted by COVID-19
* $400 million to assist local food banks to help meet increased demand during this emergency
* $250 million to Senior Nutrition programs that will provide an additional 25 million home-delivered and pre-packaged meals to low-income seniors

But in a crisis like this, we must all stand together to help those in need.

**So, friend, if you're in the place where you can donate -- contribute to[Feeding America's COVID-19 Response Fund](https://go.menendezfornj.com/page/m/74542e65/74d715b9/1af38718/1f4ab85f/2182037087/VEsD/?g=NDtPN8Qot1BcghgJFwVmbpA "https://secure.feedingamerica.org/site/Donation2;jsessionid=00000000.app20122b?28371.donation=form1&idb=155356515&df_id=28371&mfc_pref=T&NONCE_TOKEN=1F90F54D58D3F6FF47E2C9F325DF264D&s_onsite_promo=hp_slider&s_subsrc=https://www.feedingamerica.org/about-us/press-room&28371_donation=form1&s_src=W203ORGSC&s_referrer=google&_ga=2.184547238.1038545703.1584545003-1176301857.1584545003&noskip=true") or [Meals on Wheels COVID-19 Response Fund](https://go.menendezfornj.com/page/m/74542e65/74d715b9/1af38718/1f4ab840/2182037087/VEsA/?g=NDtPN8Qot1BcghgJFwVmbpA "https://ams.mealsonwheelsamerica.org/eweb/DynamicPage.aspx?WebCode=covid19donate&noskip=true") today.**

Thank you,

Bob Menendez [FEEDING AMERICA COVID-19 RESPONSE FUND](https://go.menendezfornj.com/page/m/74542e65/74d715b9/1af38718/1f4ab85f/2182037087/VEsB/?g=NDtPN8Qot1BcghgJFwVmbpA "https://secure.feedingamerica.org/site/Donation2;jsessionid=00000000.app20122b?28371.donation=form1&idb=155356515&df_id=28371&mfc_pref=T&NONCE_TOKEN=1F90F54D58D3F6FF47E2C9F325DF264D&s_onsite_promo=hp_slider&s_subsrc=https://www.feedingamerica.org/about-us/press-room&28371_donation=form1&s_src=W203ORGSC&s_referrer=google&_ga=2.184547238.1038545703.1584545003-1176301857.1584545003&noskip=true") [MEALS ON WHEELS COVID-19 RESPONSE FUND](https://go.menendezfornj.com/page/m/74542e65/74d715b9/1af38718/1f4ab840/2182037087/VEsO/?g=NDtPN8Qot1BcghgJFwVmbpA "https://ams.mealsonwheelsamerica.org/eweb/DynamicPage.aspx?WebCode=covid19donate&noskip=true")

"" "" Food banks, nursing homes, homeless shelters, and other social institutions are dealing with additional challenges as they attempt to social distance while still trying to secure food and resources. As a part of the Families First Coronavirus Response Act that was just signed into law, our government is assisting these efforts with: $500 million to WIC programs across the country to provide nutritious foods to low-income pregnant women or mothers with young children impacted by COVID-19 $400 million to assist local food banks to help meet increased demand during this emergency $250 million to Senior Nutrition programs that will provide an additional 25 million home-delivered and pre-packaged meals to low-income seniors But in a crisis like this, we must all stand together to help those in need. So, friend, if you're in the place where you can donate -- contribute to "" or "" today. Thank you, Bob Menendez "" "" | PAID FOR BY MENENDEZ FOR SENATE Contributions or gifts to Menendez for Senate are not tax deductible. PO BOX 32248 NEWARK, NJ 071020648

subject: [ACTION REQUEST] Congress Must Not Neglect Charities, Churches and Other Non-Profits in Economic Stimulus!
body:
Peter,
We are concerned that members of Congress may pass over America’s charities, churches, and other non-profit organizations in the emergency economic stimulus.
If the virus cripples charitable giving and decimates nonprofits, it will be a devastating blow to our nation’s social fabric and fracture the foundational character of our country.
Join us immediately by contacting your members of Congress and telling them, “more people will suffer if you neglect America’s charities in your economic stimulus package.” In fact, My Faith Votes will send the letter for you with our easy to use tool. Dear Friend, As the number of confirmed cases of COVID-19 continues to skyrocket, it's clear we need to: LISTEN TO OUR FRONT-LINE MEDICAL PROFESSIONALS Our medical professionals have been forecasting, and are now experiencing, a shortage of the supplies they need to protect themselves and treat those who are hospitalized with COVID-19. It's time for the President to not just invoke, but **_utilize_** the Defense Production Act and compel industries to produce the masks, face shields, gloves, ventilators, test kits, and more that our health care providers have been calling for for weeks. It takes time to acquire raw materials, re-tool production lines, and ramp up production. Our health care professionals must have the supplies they need to fight this battle - now. LISTEN TO OUR PUBLIC HEALTH OFFICIALS The number of confirmed cases of COVID-19 now exceeds 25,000. We must listen to our public health officials when they tell us that the scale of the pandemic requires broad-based social distancing measures to flatten the curve and not overload our health care systems. EVERYBODY has to participate, hopefully willingly. If not, state and local officials must mandate participation. SUPPORT THOSE WHO ARE LOSING THEIR JOBS Congress is contemplating various relief/stimulus packages. Whatever passes must bolster the unemployment insurance system to assure that the millions of Americans who are losing their jobs receive support that will allow them to pay their rent/mortgage, feed their families, and afford the medicines they need to keep themselves healthy. Again - be diligent. Protect yourself, your loved ones, and your community. Be safe and be well. We'll get through this. name: TinaforMinnesota.com
subject: Fwd: Second Harvest Heartland
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
body:
|
#
| | Did you get Tina’s message yesterday, friend? She asked supporters like you to chip in whatever you can afford to support an organization on the front lines of the coronavirus pandemic. **Second Harvest Heartland is providing food to those most affected by this public health emergency and making sure no one goes hungry — and they could really use our help today.** **[If you can, please consider adding a donation of $10 or whatever amount is meaningful to you to support Second Harvest Heartland today. Then, continue reading below for Tina’s message.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16362006/173697915/-63012667?refcode=em200321-bump&amounts=10,25,50,100,150,250,1000&firstname=&lastname=&email=&addr1=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTU1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZDhkZjk4OS1lNTZiLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=7d-2PpqlN9JW_ubA6jlcO8ISUzjWtqpYmB4KwCCSQVc=)** | [Contribute](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16362007/173697916/-63012667?refcode=em200321-bump&amounts=10,25,50,100,150,250,1000&firstname=&lastname=&email=&addr1=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTU1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZDhkZjk4OS1lNTZiLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=7d-2PpqlN9JW_ubA6jlcO8ISUzjWtqpYmB4KwCCSQVc=)
---
Friends —
As the coronavirus continues to spread, so many families are facing immeasurable hardships. On top of trying to stay safe and healthy, folks across the country are wondering how they’re going to put food on the table as more and more people lose their jobs or get sent home without pay.
**It’s an untenable position to be in, but I want you to know that I’m with you. I understand what you’re going through and I’m working my tail off in the Senate to make sure families like yours receive the assistance they deserve right now.**
In the meantime, **Second Harvest Heartland** is working their tails off, too. They’re one of the nation’s largest hunger-relief organizations fighting around the clock to ensure no Minnesota family goes hungry during this crisis.
If there’s anything I know about this incredible grassroots team, it's our willingness to lend a helping hand — especially when it matters most. And I’m emailing you now because _this is when it matters most._

It’s an untenable position to be in, but I want you to know that I’m with you. I understand what you’re going through and I’m working my tail off in the Senate to make sure families like yours receive the assistance they deserve right now. In the meantime, Second Harvest Heartland is working their tails off, too. They’re one of the nation’s largest hunger-relief organizations fighting around the clock to ensure no Minnesota family goes hungry during this crisis. And I’m emailing you now because this is when it matters most. = = Please, only give if you can afford to do so.
